type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Adding domain= parameter support to trans tag. Will edit: InternationalizationExtension.parse() (parse domain="string" param in trans tag header), InternationalizationExtension._make_node() (generate dgettext/dngettext calls when domain given), InternationalizationExtension._install_callables() (install dgettext/dngettext globals).

fields.note: Adding domain= parameter support to trans tag. Will edit: InternationalizationExtension.parse() (parse domain="string" param in trans tag header), InternationalizationExtension._make_node() (generate dgettext/dngettext calls when domain given), InternationalizationExtension._install_callables() (install dgettext/dngettext globals).

This adds 'domain' as a name+assign keyword (like a reserved variable name) inside the existing while-loop, before the generic variables[token.value]=parse_expression() fallback. It reuses the existing comma/colon/duplicate-name machinery. Please graft your 'metadata' parsing into this same skeleton (as another special-cased token.value branch, mirroring how 'domain' is done, OR alongside 'trimmed' if it's a bare flag) and send back the full combined loop so we hold identical text. Where in this loop does metadata hook in, and does it require an assign (metadata=...) or is it a bare flag?

Kept the comma-check as 'if variables:' UNCHANGED (not widened) — matches existing precedent where 'trimmed'/'notrimmed' (docs/templates.rst example: '{% trans trimmed book_title=book.title %}') don't require a comma before/after them either, since they never populate the 'variables' dict. So domain and metadata (note/created/modified) behave the same way as flags: no comma needed around them, but a comma IS required before/after real variables. This means neither of us needs to touch the comma-check line at all, reducing our overlap surface. I moved my domain-branch to be a sibling top-level 'if' (like your note/created/modified block) rather than nested inside the assign-check, for symmetry with your style. Reused your note/created/modified snippet verbatim. Please confirm this combined loop is correct for your metadata feature, or send back edits.
Agreed, byte-for-byte. Ready for the _make_node() signature/body merge and the call-site in parse() that invokes _make_node().

content:
type: DRAFT
files: src/jinja2/ext.py
region: _make_node() signature+body and its call site in parse()
code:     def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            if domain is not None:
                gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    gettext, [domain, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
                )
            else:
                gettext = nodes.Name("gettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(gettext, [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            if domain is not None:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("dngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [domain, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )
            else:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("ngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output
note: Signature appends domain and metadata as trailing optional kwargs (defaults None) so this is backward compatible for any other caller. Call site in parse() becomes: self._make_node(singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, bool(referenced), num_called_num and have_plural, domain, metadata) -- positional, in that order, then node.set_lineno(lineno) unchanged after. dgettext/dngettext globals get registered by me in _install_callables (not overlapping with your code). Let me know if you want metadata passed as dict-or-None vs always dict, and confirm the output.trans_metadata assignment placement (I put it right before return, using truthy check so empty metadata doesn't set the attr - adjust if you want it always set).

fields.code:
def _make_node(
        self,
        singular: str,
        plural: t.Optional[str],
        variables: t.Dict[str, nodes.Expr],
        plural_expr: t.Optional[nodes.Expr],
        vars_referenced: bool,
        num_called_num: bool,
        domain: t.Optional[nodes.Expr] = None,
        metadata: t.Optional[t.Dict[str, str]] = None,
    ) -> nodes.Output:
        """Generates a useful node from the data provided."""
        newstyle = self.environment.newstyle_gettext  # type: ignore
        node: nodes.Expr

        # no variables referenced?  no need to escape for old style
        # gettext invocations only if there are vars.
        if not vars_referenced and not newstyle:
            singular = singular.replace("%%", "%")
            if plural:
                plural = plural.replace("%%", "%")

        # singular only:
        if plural_expr is None:
            if domain is not None:
                gettext = nodes.Name("dgettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    gettext, [domain, nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None
                )
            else:
                gettext = nodes.Name("gettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(gettext, [nodes.Const(singular)], [], None, None)

        # singular and plural
        else:
            if domain is not None:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("dngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [domain, nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )
            else:
                ngettext = nodes.Name("ngettext", "load")
                node = nodes.Call(
                    ngettext,
                    [nodes.Const(singular), nodes.Const(plural), plural_expr],
                    [],
                    None,
                    None,
                )

        # in case newstyle gettext is used, the method is powerful
        # enough to handle the variable expansion and autoescape
        # handling itself
        if newstyle:
            for key, value in variables.items():
                # the function adds that later anyways in case num was
                # called num, so just skip it.
                if num_called_num and key == "num":
                    continue
                node.kwargs.append(nodes.Keyword(key, value))

        # otherwise do that here
        else:
            # mark the return value as safe if we are in an
            # environment with autoescaping turned on
            node = nodes.MarkSafeIfAutoescape(node)
            if variables:
                node = nodes.Mod(
                    node,
                    nodes.Dict(
                        [
                            nodes.Pair(nodes.Const(key), value)
                            for key, value in variables.items()
                        ]
                    ),
                )
        output = nodes.Output([node])
        if metadata:
            output.trans_metadata = metadata
        return output
fields.note: Signature appends domain and metadata as trailing optional kwargs (defaults None) so this is backward compatible for any other caller. Call site in parse() becomes: self._make_node(singular, plural, variables, plural_expr, bool(referenced), num_called_num and have_plural, domain, metadata) -- positional, in that order, then node.set_lineno(lineno) unchanged after. dgettext/dngettext globals get registered by me in _install_callables (not overlapping with your code). Let me know if you want metadata passed as dict-or-None vs always dict, and confirm the output.trans_metadata assignment placement (I put it right before return, using truthy check so empty metadata doesn't set the attr - adjust if you want it always set).
